title: Code Review APPROVE — money-handling boundary (POST /api/categorize amount validation)
description: Reviewed branch dev-clone/wire-amount-validation-categorize-main: replaces Number.isFinite with isValidSignedAmount at the live categorize boundary. 9/9 tests green, lint clean, no typecheck errors in changed files (only pre-existing @supabase/ssr baseline debt). No critical/security/correctness issues; net security improvement. Verdict APPROVE-with-notes. Because this boundary feeds real ledger/token-charged operations, surfacing the verdict for a human glance before merge, per team policy.

title: Security findings — ai-bookkeeper @ 0.9.0 (1 high, 3 medium, 2 low)
description: Rescan after the app gained live API routes + LLM. Triage each finding: action now, schedule, or accept-with-note. H1 (categorize prompt-injection/unbounded description) is already team-approved for fix and just needs landing. M2 (amount.ts coercion) must land before the SMS/OCR adapter. M3 (postcss) is build-only and may be accepted-with-note rather than forcing a next downgrade.

title: Approve release v0.10.0 (MINOR) — tag & publish ai-bookkeeper
description: Release PR #2 (release/v0.10.0 → main) bumps package.json 0.9.0→0.10.0 and prepends the categorized CHANGELOG entry. Bump = MINOR (one additive feature commit 6034931e; no breaking changes). The actual git tag + GitHub release are held pending your approval because Automerge is disabled per team policy and tag/publish is irreversible/outward-facing. Approving = merge PR #2, then I (or release tooling) cut the annotated tag v0.10.0 on the merge commit and publish the release notes. Note: open security findings from the 2026-06-01 scan are carried as Known Issues, not fixed here.
